input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user is dealing with a known behavioral difference between VSTest and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MSTest runner). In Microsoft.Testing.Platform, when a test assembly produces zero tests, it returns exit code 8 (which means "no tests found"), causing CI pipelines to fail. With VSTest, this was not an error.

Let me look at their project files to understand their setup.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat "no tests discovered" as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat "no tests discovered" as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
The fix works — zero tests discovered now returns **exit code 0** instead of 8.

**What happened:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code **8** when no tests are found in an assembly. VSTest treated this as success, so your CI never noticed. With the new runner, it's an explicit error.

**The fix:** Added this MSBuild property to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

This tells the test runner to treat exit code 8 ("no tests found") as success. Actual test failures (exit code 2) still fail your CI as expected.
